generated_oml:
instance AmbientTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "External temperature sensor measuring ambient conditions (T1)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureData
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensorT2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Internal temperature sensor T2"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureData
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensorT3 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Internal temperature sensor T3"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureData
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw temperature readings from three sensors transmitted via RabbitMQ message broker"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AmbientTemperatureSensor, InternalTemperatureSensorT2, InternalTemperatureSensorT3
]

instance TemperatureDataRaw : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw temperature measurements from sensors"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQ, KalmanFilter
]

instance RabbitMQ : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source message broker for inter-component communication"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTransmissionService
]

instance KalmanFilter : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kalman filter algorithm for state estimation"
    DTDFVocab:enables StateEstimationService
]

instance GitHubRepository : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Public GitHub repository for version control and software code"
    DTDFVocab:enables VersionControlService
]

instance ThermalModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Thermal model with two free parameters incorporating uniform temperature distribution, no wall heat accumulation, constant specific heat capacity, and complete power-to-heat conversion"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilter
]

instance ThermalModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Thermal model with four free parameters relaxing assumption of complete power-to-heat conversion to account for heatbed thermal inertia"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilter
]

instance DataTransmissionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for transmitting sensor data and control commands via RabbitMQ"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTransmissionAction
]

instance StateEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for estimating temperature state using Kalman filter"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureStateInsight
]

instance VersionControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for version control of software and hardware specifications"
    DTDFVocab:provides VersionControlInsight
]

instance DataTransmissionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Transmitting temperature data and control commands through RabbitMQ"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance TemperatureStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Estimated internal temperature state based on sensor data and Kalman filter"
]

instance VersionControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Tracking of software and hardware versions via GitHub repository"
]

instance Heatbed : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Heating element actuator for thermal incubator system"
]

instance Fan : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Air circulation fan actuator for uniform temperature distribution"
]

instance RaspberryPi : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Hardware platform for controller implementation running bang-bang control logic with LL, UL, H, C parameters"
]

instance LowLevelDriver : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component enforcing safety protocols and translating commands to actuator actions"
]

instance ThermalIncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Styrofoam-insulated container housing thermal incubator system components"
    base:contains Heatbed, Fan, AmbientTemperatureSensor, InternalTemperatureSensorT2, InternalTemperatureSensorT3, RaspberryPi, LowLevelDriver
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands for heatbed and fan are transmitted via RabbitMQ from digital twin components to the controller. The controller processes these commands and sends them to the low-level driver, which enforces a safe protocol for execution. Command validation occurs at the low-level driver to ensure safe operation, and feedback on command execution status is relayed back through RabbitMQ for monitoring."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system utilizes RabbitMQ as a message broker for communication between the physical twin's controller and low-level driver components. Digital twin components listen to these messages to synchronize data, incorporating Model A and Model B for temperature simulation and calibration as described in Section 3.3. The architecture is structured around this communication flow, as illustrated in Figure 7."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model validation includes testing Assumption 1 (uniform temperature distribution) in section 4. Calibration results (Figure 13) indicate Model B better predicts thermal inertia. Uncertainty quantification and error correction mechanisms are not explicitly described in the documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Communication between physical and digital components uses RabbitMQ as a message broker with a broker-based network topology. However, specific details on bandwidth, latency requirements, redundancy mechanisms, and security protocols are not provided in the documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The system is deployed on edge hardware (Raspberry Pi) running the controller, low-level driver, and RabbitMQ message broker on-premise. Computational requirements, scalability mechanisms, and cloud deployment options are not detailed in the documents."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration is achieved using RabbitMQ (AMQP protocol) as the messaging backbone for data exchange between the physical twin's low-level driver, controller, and digital twin components. The system's open-source implementation is hosted on GitHub (https://github.com/INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator), providing standardized interfaces for external system integration."
]
